title: Seeded Asteroid Generation, Polygons, Sizes, and Difficulty Inputs · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in seeded readable asteroid factory and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness.
Generate ordered angular vertices with bounded radius variation and validate minimum size, winding, and intersections.
Define large, medium, and small radius, speed, score, fragment, rendering, and collision contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Seeded Rng
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 2158 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness.

Inspect the Seeded Rng trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Seeded Rng.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Seeded Rng, keep Seeded Rng connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Difficulty Inputs
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-inputs-1k66k4" that uses case 2962 to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def evidence_asteroids_difficulty_inputs_1k66k4(vertices: int = 11, min_radius: float = 18.0, max_radius: float = 31.0, seeded_equal: bool = True, self_intersections: int = 0, spawn_budget: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1k66k4 = "1k66k4-4k66k1-2962"
    assert evidence_variant_1k66k4
    evidence_axes_1k66k4 = (
        "player1k66k4",
        "seed2962",
        "input4k66k1",
        "frame1k66k42962",
        "state29621k66k4",
        "playtest4k66k12962",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1k66k4)) == 6
    if vertices < 5 or min_radius <= 0.0 or max_radius < min_radius:
        return False, vertices, "polygon-domain"
    if not seeded_equal:
        return False, vertices, "rng-drift"
    if self_intersections > 0:
        return False, self_intersections, "invalid-polygon"
    if spawn_budget < 1:
        return False, spawn_budget, "spawn-budget"
    assert max_radius >= min_radius
    return True, spawn_budget, "seeded-asteroids"
